tables.title: Compression-Ventilation Ratios in Pediatric and Adult BLS
Chest Compression Parameters by Age Group
rows: Two-thumb encircling-hands or heel of one hand
At least 1.5 inches (4 cm) — ⅓ AP diameter
At least 2 inches (5 cm) — ⅓ AP diameter
At least 2 inches (5 cm), not exceeding 2.4 inches (6 cm)

tables.title: When to Start Compressions — Decision Thresholds
rows: Start CPR immediately (30:2 single / 15:2 team pre-pubertal)
Start CPR immediately — agonal gasps are NOT normal breathing
Agonal gasps occur in arrest; treat as pulseless
Pulse < 60 bpm + poor perfusion + adequate ventilation
HR < 60 = inadequate cardiac output in a child
Adequate cardiac output — ventilation is the priority
Poor perfusion with acceptable HR — reassess for deterioration
